mozzarella.note: Melts similarly with a slightly sharper, tangier flavor.
Very meltable and buttery; milder than mozzarella.

basil.substitute: spinach + a pinch of dried oregano
basil.note: Loses the peppery aroma but keeps the green freshness.
Adds a peppery bite instead of basil's sweetness.

heavy cream.note: Adds richness with a mild coconut flavor; common in Indian curries.
Lighter texture, less fat, slightly less rich.

yogurt.note: Similar tang and thickness, slightly higher fat.
Thinner consistency; reduce other liquids slightly.

fish sauce.substitute: soy sauce + a squeeze of lime
fish sauce.note: Loses some funk/umami depth but keeps saltiness and brightness.

parmesan.note: Saltier and sharper, made from sheep's milk.
Vegan option with a nutty, cheesy flavor, much milder.

butter.note: Nuttier flavor, higher smoke point, lactose-free.
Loses richness and browning flavor but works for moisture.

chicken thighs.note: Leaner and cooks faster; easier to overcook and dry out.
Vegetarian option; absorbs marinade well but won't shred.

anchovy fillets.note: Contains anchovy extract; adds similar umami depth in smaller amounts.

all-purpose flour.note: Designed to mimic structure; texture may be slightly denser.
